posts.hook: the cia's own spy work on an emirati ai firm backfired into more us chip access
posts.caption: a cia operative's spying on an emirati ai firm backfires into more us chip access, dave eggers tells openai staff chatgpt is silencing a generation, and oracle eats a costlier fuel-cell bill to keep its ai data center on track. full digest at sift.yasint.dev (link in bio)

slides.title: cia operative's spying on g42 ends up helping uae get more us ai chips
slides.desc: the operation meant to scrutinize export controls ended up smoothing them

terms.gloss: emirati ai firm us officials have scrutinized over china ties

slides.title: dave eggers told openai staff chatgpt is silencing an entire generation
slides.desc: the author's internal talk turned into a rebuke of the company's own product

slides.title: oracle swaps gas turbines for costlier fuel cells at its $165b data center
slides.desc: permit hurdles are pushing ai infrastructure costs up before it even runs

slides.title: 60+ app store games turn into gambling apps for brazilian users
slides.desc: a geofenced disguise built to dodge review from outside the target region

slides.title: a developer with a legal history bids $10b for california's biggest data center
slides.desc: his own past is now the central argument opponents are making against it

slides.title: alibaba open-sources chip software to chip away at nvidia's cuda lock-in
slides.desc: the third chinese chipmaker this year to open-source its software stack

terms.gloss: nvidia's software platform that locks developers into its chips
